title: Cephaelis horridula
intro: Cephaelis horridula, also known as the Horrid Yellow Cephaelis, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever and inflammation, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Cephaelis horridula include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Cephaelis horridula is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, and its extracts are also used in traditional herbal medicine to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal issues, as well as to promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Cephaelis horridula is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Cephaelis. The plant is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is a part of the large family of flowering plants known as Rubiaceae, which also includes coffee and gardenias. More specifically, it is classified as a member of the subfamily Cinchonoideae.
